methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $8.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Skyryse is a Series C aerospace company that has raised $500M+ to date, with a current post-money valuation of $1.15B. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 4 private peers in Space & Aerospace at the same stage (Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, Isar Aerospace, Loft Orbital), alongside reference to 5 public comparables in the sector (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $1.1B last post-money, implying $2.0B. Peer-median anchoring across the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.2B, though the peer range is wide ($1.0B–$8.0B, driven by Sierra Space's $8.0B outlier). Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied; the public sector median trades at 42.9x revenue, but Skyryse's revenue is not on file. Triangulating these two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The current $1.15B valuation sits near the low end. Key uncertainty remains the dispersion within the peer set and absence of revenue visibility for earnings-based validation.
